title: TC002-Provider 5-step registration flow completion
description: Verify that a service provider can complete the full 5-step registration flow without errors and with correct data submission.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Navigate to your target URL and wait until the network request is committed
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)

        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
        except async_api.Error:
            pass

        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well
        for frame in page.frames:
            try:
                await frame.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
            except async_api.Error:
                pass

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8081
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Open the same URL in a new tab to see if the SPA renders there (attempt natural navigation instead of reload). If the new tab is also blank, plan next: try reloading the original tab or report website issue.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Reload (navigate) the registration page in the current tab to force the SPA to initialize, then wait 5 seconds and re-check the page for interactive elements (forms, buttons, links).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Reload the registration page in the current tab (navigate to http://localhost:8081) to force the SPA to initialize. After navigation, check for interactive elements; if still blank, prepare to report a website issue.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # -> Final assertions based on the rendered page content (JavaScript disabled message)
        assert await page.is_visible("text=You need to enable JavaScript to run this app."), "Expected the JavaScript-required message to be visible; the SPA did not initialize.",
        # -> Verify that registration form elements are not present when JavaScript is disabled
        selectors = [
            'form#provider-registration',
            'input[name="email"]',
            'input[type="email"]',
            'input[name="password"]',
            'input[name="name"]',
            'button[type="submit"]',
            'text=Register',
            'text=Sign up',
            'text=Create account'
        ]
        for sel in selectors:
            count = await page.locator(sel).count()
            assert count == 0, f"Expected no element matching '{sel}' when JavaScript is disabled, but found {count}"
        
        # -> Because the SPA did not initialize, we cannot proceed with the 5-step registration flow. Fail the test explicitly to highlight the environment issue.
        raise AssertionError('Registration flow could not be executed: JavaScript appears to be disabled or the SPA failed to initialize.')
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC007-Add, edit, delete, and toggle service availability by provider
description: Verify providers can manage their service menu correctly: add new services, edit existing ones, delete services, and activate/deactivate availability with immediate updates reflected in client listings.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Navigate to your target URL and wait until the network request is committed
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)

        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
        except async_api.Error:
            pass

        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well
        for frame in page.frames:
            try:
                await frame.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
            except async_api.Error:
                pass

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8081
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Reload the page by navigating to http://localhost:8081 to attempt to load the SPA (refresh).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Open the app in a new tab (navigate to http://localhost:8081 in a new tab) to attempt loading the SPA from a fresh context.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Open a new tab to http://localhost:8081 with a cache-bypass query parameter to force the SPA to load (fresh context).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/?_cb=1707321600", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Attempt direct navigation to the login route to force the SPA to load (navigate to http://localhost:8081/login). If that fails, try alternate routes or report website issue.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Open the app using an alternate host to bypass any hostname binding issue: navigate to http://127.0.0.1:8081/login in a new tab and check for interactive elements.
        await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:8081/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC013-Handle service management edge case: editing service with invalid data
description: Verify that the service management screens validate inputs when editing a service and do not allow saving invalid data (e.g., empty title, negative price).
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Navigate to your target URL and wait until the network request is committed
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)

        # Wait for the main page to reach DOMContentLoaded state (optional for stability)
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
        except async_api.Error:
            pass

        # Iterate through all iframes and wait for them to load as well
        for frame in page.frames:
            try:
                await frame.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=3000)
            except async_api.Error:
                pass

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:8081
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Reload the app by navigating to http://localhost:8081 to attempt to load the React Native Web SPA. If the page still has no interactive elements, try a different navigation approach next (open in new tab or report issue).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Open the app in a new tab (http://localhost:8081) to attempt loading the SPA and then inspect the new tab for interactive elements (login or navigation).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Open the app using a hash-based SPA URL (http://localhost:8081/#/) in a new tab to attempt to load the React Native Web app and check for interactive elements (login or navigation).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/#/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to the app's login path (/login) to attempt to load the SPA and check for interactive elements (login form) on that page.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Open alternate entrypoint http://localhost:8081/index.html in a new tab and check the page for interactive elements (login or navigation).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:8081/index.html", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
